title: My Life in China and America
description: My Life in China and America — knowledge fragments for AI agents

memes.label: About This Wiki
memes.description: A knowledge wiki for "My Life in China and America" — this page is auto-generated from book metadata

memes.label: "Appendix"
memes.description: "Address by Rev. Joseph H. Twichell on Yung Wing and the Chinese Educational Mission, delivered at Yale Law School, April 10, 1878."

memes.label: "Imprint"

memes.label: "Preface"
memes.description: "Yung Wing's preface, written in Hartford, Connecticut in November 1909, outlining the structure and purpose of his autobiography."

memes.label: 'Table of Contents'
memes.description: 'Table of Contents'

memes.label: "My Life in China and America"
memes.description: "Title page of Yung Wing's autobiography, \"My Life in China and America,\" published in 1909."

memes.label: "Uncopyright"
memes.description: "CC0 public domain dedication and license information."

memes.label: Benjamin Hobson
memes.description: The British medical missionary in Macao who fulfilled Mrs. Gutzlaffs dying wish by finding Yung Wing and placing him in the Morrison Education Society School.

memes.label: Boxer Rebellion
memes.description: The 1899–1901 anti-foreign uprising that represented the violent rejection of Western influence Yung Wing had sought to manage through education.

memes.label: Canton
memes.description: The southern treaty port and historic center of foreign trade in China.

memes.label: "Chapter I: Boyhood"
memes.description: "Yung Wing's early childhood in Nam Ping village near Macao, his admission to Mrs. Gutzlaff's mission school, and his first encounter with Western education."

memes.label: "Chapter II: School Days"
memes.description: "Yung Wing enters the Morrison Education Society School under Rev. S. R. Brown, forming the first class of Chinese students to receive English education in China."

memes.label: "Chapter III: Journey to America and First Experiences There"
memes.description: "The 98-day voyage from Whompoa to New York via St. Helena, and first impressions of America."

memes.label: "Chapter IV: At Monson Academy"
memes.description: "Yung Wing and his companions enter Monson Academy in Massachusetts under the care of Rev. Charles Hammond, preparing for college."

memes.label: "Chapter V: My College Days"
memes.description: "Yung Wing's years at Yale College (1850–1854), struggling with poverty while excelling in English composition and forming his vision for China's educational reform."

memes.label: "Chapter VI: Return to China"
memes.description: "After eight years in America, Yung Wing returns to China in 1855, confronting the stark contrast between Western civilization and his homeland."

memes.label: "Chapter VII: Effort to Find a Position"
memes.description: "Yung Wing struggles to find suitable employment in China, mastering the spoken language and seeking a platform for reform."

memes.label: "Chapter VIII: Experiences in Business"
memes.description: "Working as a clerk in an English tea and silk firm, gaining mercantile knowledge, and encounters with corruption in the Imperial Customs service."

memes.label: "Chapter IX: My First Trip to the Tea Districts"
memes.description: "A journey through the canal and lake systems of southern Kiangsu to the green tea districts, observing Chinese inland commerce."

memes.label: "Chapter X: My Visit to the Taipings"
memes.description: "In 1859, Yung Wing travels to Nanking to assess the Taiping rebels, meeting their leaders and evaluating their capacity to reform China."

memes.label: "Chapter XI: Reflections on the Taiping Rebellion"
memes.description: "Analysis of the Taiping Rebellion's causes, character, and prospects, concluding that the Taipings are incapable of genuine reform."

memes.label: "Chapter XII: Expedition to the Taiping Tea District"
memes.description: "A commercial venture into rebel-held territory to procure green tea, navigating dangers and building business experience."

memes.label: "Chapter XIII: My Interviews with Tsang Kwoh Fan"
memes.description: "Summoned by Viceroy Tsang Kwoh Fan, Yung Wing proposes establishing a machine shop — the seed of China's first modern arsenal."

memes.label: "Chapter XIV: My Mission to America to Buy Machinery"
memes.description: "Commissioned with the fifth official rank, Yung Wing travels to America to purchase machinery for the Kiangnan Arsenal."

memes.label: "Chapter XV: My Second Return to China"
memes.description: "Returning via San Francisco and circumnavigating the globe, Yung Wing delivers the machinery and is promoted by the Viceroy."

memes.label: "Chapter XVI: Proposal of My Educational Scheme"
memes.description: "Yung Wing formalizes his plan to send Chinese students to America for Western education, persuading Tsang Kwoh Fan to memorialize the throne."

memes.label: "Chapter XVII: The Chinese Educational Mission"
memes.description: "The Chinese Educational Mission is established in Hartford, Connecticut, with Yung Wing and Chin Lan Pin as co-commissioners, bringing 120 students to America in annual detachments."

memes.label: "Chapter XVIII: Investigation of the Coolie Traffic in Peru"
memes.description: "Yung Wing is dispatched to Peru to investigate the abuse of Chinese coolie laborers, resulting in a treaty to protect Chinese emigrants."

memes.label: "Chapter XIX: End of the Educational Mission"
memes.description: "Conservative opposition and internal sabotage lead to the abrupt recall of all students and the dissolution of the Chinese Educational Mission in 1881."

memes.label: "Chapter XX: Journey to Peking and Death of My Wife"
memes.description: "Yung Wing travels to Peking seeking redress for the Mission's closure, enduring personal tragedy with the death of his wife Mary Kellogg."

memes.label: "Chapter XXI: My Recall to China"
memes.description: "During the Sino-Japanese War of 1894–95, Yung Wing proposes financial and military reforms, leading to his recall to China for government service."

memes.label: "Chapter XXII: The Coup d'État of 1898"
memes.description: "Yung Wing witnesses the Hundred Days' Reform and its violent suppression by Empress Dowager Cixi, narrowly escaping execution and fleeing to Shanghai."

memes.label: Theodore Dwight Woolsey
memes.description: The President of Yale College during Yung Wings student years who recognized his academic achievements.

memes.label: Chin Lan Pin
memes.description: The Qing official who served as co-commissioner of the Chinese Educational Mission alongside Yung Wing.

memes.label: Chinese Diaspora
memes.description: The global dispersal of Chinese populations in the 19th century, including students, laborers, and merchants.

memes.label: Chinese Educational Commission
memes.description: The official Qing government commission that administered the Chinese Educational Mission in the United States from 1872 to 1881, co-directed by Chin Lan Pin and Yung Wing.
aliases: Board of Commissioners for the Educational Mission

memes.label: Chinese Educational Mission
memes.description: Yung Wings pioneering 1872–1881 program that sent 120 Chinese students to study in the United States — the first organized Chinese educational exchange with the West and the cornerstone of his life's work.
aliases: Chinese Educational Mission to the United States

memes.label: Chinese Government Telegraph Administration
memes.description: The modern telecommunications agency that Yung Wing helped establish during the Self-Strengthening era.

memes.label: Christian Missions in China
memes.description: The network of Protestant and Catholic missionary institutions — schools, hospitals, and churches — that served as the primary conduit for Western education in 19th-century China and shaped Yung Wings formative years.

memes.label: Confucian Education
memes.description: The traditional Chinese education system based on the Confucian classics, which dominated Chinese intellectual life for centuries and served as the sole path to official advancement before modern reforms.

memes.label: Coolie Investigation in Peru
memes.description: Yung Wings 1873 diplomatic mission to Peru to investigate the conditions of Chinese indentured laborers.

memes.label: Coolie Trade
memes.description: The system of exporting Chinese indentured laborers under coercive conditions, which Yung Wing investigated in Peru on behalf of the Qing government.

memes.label: Death of Tsang Kwoh Fan
memes.description: The 1872 death of Viceroy Tsang Kwoh Fan, which removed the Educational Missions most powerful protector.

memes.label: East India Company
memes.description: The British trading monopoly that controlled the opium trade and dominated Sino-British commerce.

memes.label: Establishment of the Chinese Educational Mission
memes.description: The 1870 imperial approval and 1872 launch of Yung Wings educational scheme that sent 120 Chinese students to study in America — the culmination of over a decade of advocacy.
aliases: Establishment of the Chinese Educational Mission

memes.label: Extraterritoriality
memes.description: The legal principle by which foreign nationals in 19th-century China were subject to their own nations laws rather than Chinese law, a key feature of the unequal treaty system.

memes.label: First Opium War
memes.description: The 1839–1842 conflict between Britain and China that opened the treaty port era and created the conditions for Yung Wings unique life path.

memes.label: Hartford Connecticut
memes.description: The Connecticut city that hosted the headquarters of the Chinese Educational Mission and became Yung Wings American home.

memes.label: Hong Kong
memes.description: The British colony adjacent to Macao that became a crucial hub for Chinese-Western exchange.

memes.label: Hong Kong Cession
memes.description: The 1842 cession of Hong Kong Island to Britain under the Treaty of Nanking, creating a crucial British foothold on Chinas coast.

memes.label: Hundred Days Reform
memes.description: The 1898 reform movement in which Yung Wing was peripherally involved, ending in the Coup dÉtat that forced him to flee Peking.

memes.label: Imperial Examination System
memes.description: The meritocratic examination system that selected Chinese government officials based on mastery of Confucian classics, abolished in 1905.

memes.label: Imperial Maritime Customs Service
memes.description: The Qing governments foreign-managed customs agency, a model of efficient Western-style administration.

memes.label: Kan Wang
memes.description: The Shield King of the Taiping Rebellion who received Yung Wing at Nanking and considered his reform proposals.

memes.label: Kiangnan Arsenal
memes.description: The major modern arsenal and shipyard established by the Self-Strengthening Movement in Shanghai.

memes.label: Ladies Association in London
memes.description: The British womens missionary organization that sponsored Mrs. Gutzlaffs school in Macao.

memes.label: Li Hung Chang
memes.description: The powerful Qing Viceroy, diplomat, and modernizer who succeeded Tsang Kwoh Fan as the leading advocate of reform and became the political gatekeeper of the Chinese Educational Mission in its final years.

memes.label: List of Events
memes.description: An index of all event pages in the wiki, sorted by start date. Covers key historical events from Yung Wing's life and 19th-century Chinese history.

memes.label: List of Organizations
memes.description: An index of all organization pages in the wiki, sorted alphabetically by label. Covers institutions, government bodies, missions, and companies significant to Yung Wing's life and 19th-century China.

memes.label: List of Persons
memes.description: An index of all person pages in the wiki, sorted alphabetically by label.

memes.label: List of Places
memes.description: An index of all place pages in the wiki, sorted alphabetically by label. Covers locations significant to Yung Wing's life and the 19th-century Sino-American world.

memes.label: London Missionary Society
memes.description: The Protestant missionary society whose members established hospitals and schools in 19th-century China.

memes.label: Macao
memes.description: The Portuguese colony on the southern coast of China where Yung Wing was born nearby and received his first education.

memes.label: Marie Yung
memes.description: The American wife of Yung Wing, whom he married during his years in Hartford, Connecticut.

memes.label: Modern Chinese Nationalism
memes.description: The emergence of modern national consciousness in China during the late Qing period, to which Yung Wings writings contributed.

memes.label: Monson Academy
memes.description: The Massachusetts preparatory school where Yung Wing studied before entering Yale College.

memes.label: Morrison Brown Yung
memes.description: The elder son of Yung Wing, named after the Morrison Education Society that gave his father his start.

memes.label: Morrison Education Society School
memes.description: The missionary school in Macao and Hong Kong that provided Yung Wing his formative Western education.

memes.label: Mrs. Karl Gutzlaff
memes.description: The English missionary who established the first foreign school Yung Wing attended in Macao, profoundly influencing the course of his life.

memes.label: Nam Ping
memes.description: The village on Pedro Island where Yung Wing was born in 1828.

memes.label: Nanking
memes.description: The former imperial capital that served as the headquarters of the Taiping Heavenly Kingdom, where Yung Wing visited in 1860 and where his patron Tsang Kwoh Fan later established his viceregal seat.

memes.label: New England
memes.description: Northeastern region of the United States where Yung Wing received his education and where the Chinese Educational Mission students were placed with American families.

memes.label: New Haven
memes.description: The Connecticut city home to Yale College where Yung Wing spent his college years from 1850 to 1854.

memes.label: Opium Trade in China
memes.description: The British-controlled opium trade that devastated 19th-century China, fueled two wars, and formed the backdrop of Yung Wing's early life.

memes.label: Pedro Island
memes.description: The island west of Macao where Yung Wing was born in the village of Nam Ping.

memes.label: Peking
memes.description: The imperial capital of Qing China where Yung Wing negotiated with the highest levels of government.

memes.label: Peru
memes.description: The South American nation where Yung Wing investigated the conditions of Chinese coolie laborers in 1873.

memes.label: Project Gutenberg
memes.description: The digital library project whose transcription of Yung Wings autobiography served as the source for modern editions.

memes.label: Qing Imperial Government
memes.description: The ruling Manchu dynasty of China during Yung Wings lifetime, which both enabled and ultimately terminated his educational mission.

memes.label: Reform Club of Peking
memes.description: The reformist political circle in Peking that Yung Wing engaged with during the 1898 reform period.

memes.label: Charles Gutzlaff
memes.description: The Prussian missionary, translator, and controversial figure whose work in China preceded that of his wife Mrs. Gutzlaff.

memes.label: Robert Morrison
memes.description: First Protestant missionary to China (1807), author of the first Anglo-Chinese dictionary and Chinese translation of the Bible, and namesake of the Morrison Education Society.

memes.label: Samuel Robins Brown
memes.description: The American missionary and teacher who led the Morrison Education Society School and prepared Yung Wing for study at Yale College.

memes.label: San Francisco
memes.description: Major Pacific port city where Yung Wing transited during his 1864 circumnavigation of the globe, en route from New York back to China.

memes.label: Second Opium War
memes.description: The 1856–1860 conflict that further opened China to foreign influence, during which Yung Wing was studying in America.

memes.label: Self-Strengthening Movement
memes.description: The 1860s–1890s Qing reform movement that sought to modernize China's military and industry by adopting Western technology while preserving Confucian values.

memes.label: Shanghai
memes.description: The premier treaty port of late imperial China and the city that served as Yung Wings commercial base, point of departure for his journeys, and the locus of key encounters during his career.

memes.label: First Sino-Japanese War
memes.description: The 1894–1895 war that exposed the limitations of the Self-Strengthening Movement and accelerated demands for reform.

memes.label: Standard Ebooks
memes.description: The volunteer-driven digital publishing project that produced the modern ebook edition of Yung Wings autobiography.

memes.label: Taiping Tea District Expedition
memes.description: Yung Wings bold commercial expedition into Taiping-controlled territory in 1860 to procure green tea, which preceded his historic visit to the Taiping capital at Nanking.

memes.label: Taiping Heavenly Kingdom
memes.description: The rebel state established by the Taiping Rebellion that controlled much of southern China from 1851 to 1864.

memes.label: Taiping Rebellion
memes.description: The massive civil war (1850–1864) that nearly toppled the Qing dynasty, which Yung Wing personally investigated during his visit to the Taiping capital at Nanking.

memes.label: Chinese Tea Districts
memes.description: The inland tea-growing regions that Yung Wing visited during his business career.

memes.label: Termination of the Educational Mission
memes.description: The 1881 recall of all 120 Chinese Educational Mission students, ending Yung Wings life work prematurely.

memes.label: Tientsin
memes.description: The northern treaty port near Peking where Yung Wing negotiated with Qing officials.

memes.label: Treaty of Nanking
memes.description: The 1842 treaty that ended the First Opium War, ceded Hong Kong to Britain, opened five treaty ports, and marked the beginning of China's "Century of Humiliation."

memes.label: Treaty Port System
memes.description: The network of Chinese ports opened to foreign trade and residence under the unequal treaties, which became centers of modernization and cultural exchange.

memes.label: Tsang Kwoh Fan
memes.description: The Viceroy and leading Qing statesman who became Yung Wings most powerful patron, secured imperial approval for the Chinese Educational Mission, and laid the foundation for the Self-Strengthening Movement.

memes.label: Unequal Treaties
memes.description: The series of 19th-century treaties imposed on China by Western powers and Japan that granted unilateral privileges to foreign nations.

memes.label: Office of the Viceroy of Zhili
memes.description: The most powerful regional governorship of the Qing empire, controlling the province surrounding the capital — held successively by Yung Wings patrons Tsang Kwoh Fan and Li Hung Chang.
aliases: Office of the Viceroy of Zhili

memes.label: Wong Foon
memes.description: One of the first three Chinese students to study in America with Yung Wing, who later became the first Chinese graduate of the University of Edinburgh medical school.

memes.label: Wong Shing
memes.description: One of the first three Chinese students brought to America by S. R. Brown alongside Yung Wing, later a prominent figure in Hong Kong.

memes.label: Yale College
memes.description: The Ivy League university where Yung Wing became the first Chinese graduate in 1854.

memes.label: Yale College Curriculum
memes.description: The mid-19th-century liberal arts curriculum at Yale College that shaped Yung Wings intellectual development.

memes.label: Yung Wing (Rong Hong, 容闳)
memes.description: First Chinese graduate of an American university (Yale College, 1854), Commissioner of the Chinese Educational Mission, and author of "My Life in China and America."

memes.label: Yung Wing Company
memes.description: Yung Wings business ventures that sustained him financially between his educational and government work.

memes.label: Yung Wing Father
memes.description: The father of Yung Wing who made the unusual decision to send his son to a foreign school, setting the course for Yung Wings extraordinary life.

memes.label: Yung Wings First Journey to America
memes.description: The 1847 voyage that brought Yung Wing and two classmates to the United States with S. R. Brown, beginning the first organized Chinese student migration to America.
aliases: Yung Wings First Journey to America

memes.label: Yung Wings Mission to Buy Machinery
memes.description: Yung Wings 1863–1864 trip to America on behalf of Tsang Kwoh Fan to purchase machinery for the Kiangnan Arsenal.
aliases: Yung Wings Mission to Buy Machinery

memes.label: Yung Wing Mother
memes.description: The resilient mother of Yung Wing who raised four children alone after her husbands death in 1840.

memes.label: Yung Wings First Return to China
memes.description: Yung Wings difficult re-entry into Chinese society in 1855 after eight years in America.
aliases: Yung Wings First Return to China

memes.label: Yung Wings Yale Commencement
memes.description: Yung Wings graduation from Yale College in 1854, making him the first Chinese graduate of an American university.

memes.label: Yung Wings Visit to the Taipings
memes.description: Yung Wings 1860 journey to Nanking to assess the Taiping Rebellion and present reform proposals.
aliases: Yung Wings Visit to the Taipings
